fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 43-year-old African American male with schizophrenia with residual symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Harris attended appointment with case manager. Case manager confirms he takes quetiapine 400 mg nightly via supervised administration at group home, no missed doses. Despite reliable adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptoms. Reports daily auditory hallucinations of voices making derogatory comments. Endorses vague paranoid ideas that people watch him. Sleep disrupted at 5 hours nightly. Appetite fair, weight stable at 191 lbs. Attends day program sporadically. Not working, receives disability. Lives in group home. Minimal social interaction. Mood 'same as always' rated 4/10. Denies substance use. Flat throughout interview. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Carol Johnson, LMSW, Social Worker)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged male, adequate grooming, appears withdrawn

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizophrenia with persistent positive and negative symptoms.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives in group home; unemployed, on disability; minimal engagement; case management services.
Assessment_and_Plan: Good adherence via group home supervision.
Will consider dose increase or medication change.
fields.Author: Note written by Carol Johnson, LMSW, Social Worker — Group Home Network, 5623 Residential Avenue, Philadelphia, PA 19102, Phone: (555) 229-7643, Fax: (555) 229-7644
